findings.title: canTrigger has 6 parameters -- consider a config/context object
findings.description: The canTrigger function accepts 6 positional parameters. This exceeds the recommended threshold of 5 and forces callers to remember the exact argument order (e.g., 'currentHour' vs 'now' are both numbers, easy to swap). Grouping the context parameters into a single object would improve call-site readability and make the function easier to extend.

findings.title: evaluateTriggers has 5 parameters -- borderline for clarity
findings.description: evaluateTriggers sits at 5 parameters (the P2 threshold). Three of them (lastTriggerTimes, now, cooldownMultiplier) are evaluation context that travels together. Grouping these into an options object would keep the signature stable when more evaluation context is inevitably needed (e.g., DND state integration).

findings.title: ONE_HOUR_MS defined in two separate modules
findings.description: ONE_HOUR_MS and TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS are independently defined in both proactive-trigger.ts (lines 10, 16) and do-not-disturb.ts (lines 3-4) with identical values. While the "three similar lines > premature abstraction" guideline applies to logic, shared named constants are a straightforward extract. A single time-constants module would eliminate the duplication and ensure consistency if values ever change.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
// proactive-trigger.ts
const ONE_HOUR_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000
const THIRTY_MINUTES_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000
const TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS = 24 * ONE_HOUR_MS

// do-not-disturb.ts
const ONE_HOUR_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000
const TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS = 24 * ONE_HOUR_MS

AFTER:
// time-constants.ts
export const ONE_HOUR_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000
export const THIRTY_MINUTES_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000
export const TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS = 24 * ONE_HOUR_MS

WHY: Eliminates duplication across two modules, ensures a single source of truth for time constants within the persona-engine package.

findings.title: canTrigger could use early returns instead of if-else chain
findings.description: This is already well-structured with guard clauses. The only minor simplification is that the final frequency check assigns to a temporary variable used once. Inlining it marginally reduces cognitive load, though the current form is perfectly readable. This is a very minor observation.

positive_observations: All functions are short (under 30 lines), with consistently low cyclomatic complexity (1-4 per function)
Excellent use of early returns and guard clauses throughout both proactive-trigger.ts and do-not-disturb.ts
Named constants are used consistently instead of magic numbers for all time durations
The NOT_TRIGGERED constant avoids repeated object allocation and communicates intent
TriggerResult is a well-designed discriminated union making illegal states unrepresentable
Immutable state pattern in do-not-disturb.ts -- all state transitions return new objects
Test helper functions (makeTriggerInput, stateWith) effectively reduce test boilerplate with clean partial-override patterns
Clear separation: trigger conditions are pure data + pure check functions, no side effects
